candidates.name: AgentLedger - Verifiable Agent Decision Attestation

primitives.name: Odra Framework
primitives.status_quote: Odra 2.8.0 released June 11 2026, actively maintained with llms.txt AI support

primitives.name: x402 Protocol
primitives.status_quote: Production on mainnet, free facilitator for hackathon, Go and TypeScript SDKs

primitives.name: Casper MCP Server
primitives.status_quote: Version 3.0.0, 82 tools for blockchain queries, supports stdio and HTTP

copilot_queries: agent decision attestation on-chain blockchain audit trail
AI agent accountability verifiable proof smart contract
EU AI Act agent explainability requirements blockchain

accelerator_only.query: agent attestation audit trail Casper hackathon
accelerator_only.zero_match_note: No existing projects on Casper or DoraHacks address agent decision attestation or on-chain audit trails for AI agents

winners_only.query: previous Casper hackathon winners agent accountability

matches.slug: Shroud Protocol - privacy mixer with ZK

comparable_matches.differentiation: EAS attests arbitrary data schemas but not specifically agent decisions. AgentLedger is purpose-built for AI agent accountability with structured decision proofs.
DECO attests off-chain data into oracles. AgentLedger attests agent DECISIONS not data feeds. Different trust model - proving reasoning not facts.
Lit provides programmable signing and encryption. AgentLedger records decision audit trails. Lit could be a signing layer under AgentLedger but does not provide attestation semantics.

weakening_source.summary: Vitalik argues most crypto+AI intersections are overhyped. On-chain attestation for AI decisions may be premature since the agent economy is still nascent.

candidates.biggest_reason_not_to_build: The AI agent economy is nascent with limited real deployments. Building attestation infrastructure for a market that may not exist in production scale yet is a timing risk.

candidates.name: AgentPay - Pay-Per-Result AI Services

primitives.name: x402 Protocol
primitives.status_quote: Production on mainnet, escrow-capable via CEP-18 tokens, TypeScript SDK available

primitives.name: Odra Framework
primitives.status_quote: Odra 2.8.0 supports state machines and token handling for escrow patterns

copilot_queries: pay per result AI service escrow blockchain
outcome based pricing AI agent micropayment

accelerator_only.query: pay per result AI escrow Casper DoraHacks
accelerator_only.zero_match_note: No existing pay-per-result AI service with on-chain escrow found on Casper ecosystem or DoraHacks submissions

winners_only.query: previous hackathon winners pay per result AI

comparable_matches.differentiation: Morpheus provides decentralized compute marketplace. AgentPay focuses on result-based payment not compute time - pay for outcomes not resources.
Bittensor rewards AI model quality via subnet mining. AgentPay is user-facing escrow for individual tasks - different market layer.
Ritual verifies AI inference on-chain computationally. AgentPay verifies USER SATISFACTION with results. Human approval loop is the differentiator.

weakening_source.summary: Quality verification for AI outputs is inherently subjective. On-chain escrow cannot resolve disputes about output quality without an oracle or arbitration layer.

candidates.biggest_reason_not_to_build: Subjective quality assessment makes escrow disputes unresolvable on-chain without costly arbitration. Users can always reject results unfairly, creating adverse selection.

candidates.name: CompliAgent - Compliance-First Agent for Regulated Operations

primitives.name: Odra Framework
primitives.status_quote: Odra supports conditional logic and access control patterns needed for whitelist and compliance checks

copilot_queries: compliance aware AI agent KYC blockchain regulation
regulated asset transfer whitelist smart contract Casper

accelerator_only.query: compliance agent regulated operations Casper blockchain
accelerator_only.zero_match_note: No compliance-focused agent projects found in Casper ecosystem or hackathon submissions. This is a complete gap.

winners_only.query: previous hackathon compliance regulation blockchain agent
winners_only.zero_match_note: No previous Casper or crypto hackathon winners focused specifically on compliance-aware agent operations

comparable_matches.differentiation: Chainalysis monitors transactions after execution. CompliAgent checks compliance BEFORE execution, preventing violations rather than detecting them.
Securitize tokenizes regulated assets with compliance built in. CompliAgent makes AI AGENTS comply - the agent layer not the token layer.
Tokeny implements ERC-3643 token compliance. CompliAgent extends this to autonomous agent operations - agents checking compliance before acting.

weakening_source.summary: MiCA compliance requirements are still being interpreted. Building compliance tooling for unclear regulations risks building the wrong checks entirely.

candidates.biggest_reason_not_to_build: Regulatory requirements for AI agents are undefined. Building compliance checks without clear regulatory standards means guessing what rules to enforce. Risk of complete irrelevance.

candidates.name: PayGate - x402 Agent Service Marketplace

primitives.name: x402 Protocol
primitives.status_quote: Production mainnet, free facilitator for hackathon, supports exact payment scheme with CEP-18 tokens

copilot_queries: AI agent marketplace service discovery x402 micropayment
agent to agent API marketplace blockchain registry

accelerator_only.query: agent API marketplace x402 Casper ecosystem
accelerator_only.zero_match_note: No agent service marketplace exists in Casper ecosystem. CSPR.trade provides DEX but not general agent service registry.

winners_only.query: previous hackathon agent marketplace service discovery

comparable_matches.differentiation: Akash provides compute marketplace for containers. PayGate provides API marketplace for AI agent services - higher abstraction, per-request pricing.
Ocean trades datasets. PayGate trades agent API services - live computation not static data. Different consumption model.
SingularityNET is Ethereum-based AI marketplace since 2017. PayGate uses x402 HTTP-native payments on Casper - simpler integration model.

weakening_source.summary: SingularityNET has existed since 2017 with similar marketplace vision and struggled with adoption. Agent service marketplaces may be solutions looking for problems.

candidates.biggest_reason_not_to_build: Agent service marketplaces have been tried before (SingularityNET since 2017) with limited adoption. Building supply infrastructure without demand is a classic chicken-and-egg problem.

candidates.name: AgentGuard - Smart Contract Security Auditor
copilot_queries: AI smart contract security auditor automated detection
Odra Rust smart contract audit tool blockchain

accelerator_only.query: smart contract auditor agent Casper Odra
accelerator_only.zero_match_note: No automated audit tools exist specifically for Odra/Casper smart contracts. Solidity tools do not apply to Rust/WASM.

winners_only.query: hackathon winners smart contract security audit tool
winners_only.zero_match_note: Security audit tools rarely win hackathons as they are utilities not products. No precedent for this category winning.

comparable_matches.differentiation: Slither is Solidity-only. AgentGuard would target Odra/Rust on Casper - different language, different patterns, different ecosystem entirely.
Mythril analyzes EVM bytecode. Odra compiles to WASM which is a completely different target with different tooling requirements.
Aderyn targets Solidity. AgentGuard targets Rust/Odra which has different issue classes like ownership bugs and unsafe blocks.

weakening_source.summary: The Odra ecosystem has very few deployed contracts. Building an audit tool for an ecosystem with perhaps a few dozen contracts means a tiny addressable market.

candidates.biggest_reason_not_to_build: The Casper Odra ecosystem has very few smart contracts deployed. Audit tooling for dozens of contracts when Solidity tools serve millions makes this a niche tool with no meaningful market.

candidates.name: AgentDAO - Autonomous Agent Collective Governance
copilot_queries: autonomous agent DAO collective governance blockchain
multi-agent voting decision system smart contract

accelerator_only.query: agent DAO autonomous governance Casper hackathon
accelerator_only.zero_match_note: No agent-DAO projects found in Casper ecosystem or hackathon submissions. The concept is theoretical with no implementations.

winners_only.query: hackathon winners agent DAO autonomous governance
winners_only.zero_match_note: No hackathon winners have built agent-governed DAOs. Regular DAO tooling wins occasionally but agent-DAOs have zero precedent.

comparable_matches.differentiation: Aragon provides DAO tooling for human governance. AgentDAO extends governance to autonomous AI agents which is a fundamentally different participant model.
Snapshot enables human voting off-chain. AgentDAO enables agent voting with automated on-chain execution. Agents both propose and execute.
AI Arena pits agents against each other competitively. AgentDAO makes agents cooperate collectively. Competition vs cooperation model.

weakening_source.summary: Multi-agent governance assumes agents have divergent preferences worth voting on. Current AI agents are tools, not autonomous entities with preferences.

candidates.biggest_reason_not_to_build: Agent governance assumes agents have preferences worth aggregating. Current AI agents are tools executing instructions, not autonomous entities needing collective decision-making.
